title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that the differentiator in agent systems is not frameworks, orchestration, or tools, but the session log itself. As models commoditize and infrastructure layers become interchangeable, durable append-only logs should become the primitive for agents, enabling durability, continuity, forkability, and portability across models and runtimes. The talk questions who controls agents when the model provider controls the log.
writeup: Ishaan Sehgal of Omnara argues that the real differentiator in agent systems is the session log, not frameworks, orchestration, or tools. The premise: as models commoditize and infrastructure layers become interchangeable, a durable append-only log becomes the core primitive, supplying durability, continuity, forkability, and portability across models and runtimes. The talk frames a control question: when the model provider owns the log, it effectively owns the agent. Concepts covered include append-only logs, multi-agent systems, session state, durability, portability, and observability.
